purpose: G005 preparation only: public game/action datasets that may be mixed with D2E after D2E-only hard gates finish.

user_decision.interpretation: D2E+aux may be the best/primary final model, but D2E-only gates and D2E-only vs D2E+aux ablations remain mandatory and separately reported.

claim_boundary.summary: This is a source-selection and storage plan, not model-quality or harness evidence.

storage_policy.cache_warning: Source files fit the 5TiB cap, but decoded caches/checkpoints must be staged and garbage-collected during G005.

recommended_curriculum: Validate each selected aux loader with source-specific train/val/test split and provenance hash.
Pretrain shared visual-temporal backbone on selected aux with source-specific action heads.
Finetune on D2E-only train split; never use aux examples inside D2E heldout splits.
Compare D2E-only vs D2E+aux under the same temporal, heldout-recording, and heldout-game D2E eval manifests.
training_policy.action_mapping_rule: Keep source-specific action heads/tokens and report source-specific metrics; only D2E keyboard/mouse endpoints can support D2E desktop-action claims.

candidates.name: MineRL 2019 Datasets backup
candidates.published_size_note: Zenodo files total 8.6 GB decimal across four MineRL-v0 zips.
candidates.supervision: human players playing Minecraft; video feed and actions captured/stored
candidates.action_signal: state-action demonstrations with Minecraft camera/button/control fields via MineRL data tooling
candidates.adapter_strategy: Use a Minecraft-specific action head and map camera/buttons into a provenance-preserving aux token namespace; do not collapse into D2E keyboard/mouse metrics.
candidates.g005_role: high-transfer first-person keyboard/mouse-like auxiliary pretraining before D2E finetune/ablation
candidates.why: Closest small, license-permissive public game/action corpus among selected candidates; complements D2E with 3D first-person control.
risks: MineRL-v0 environment/action schema differs from D2E desktop MCAP keyboard/mouse events
needs adapter/token mapping and source-specific heads
Minecraft IP/provenance notices must remain separate from D2E artifacts
source_evidence: Zenodo description says the record backs up parts of MineRL 2019 and contains human Minecraft play with video feed and actions captured/stored.
MineRL GitHub README links the Zenodo backup after original mirrors went down and describes data access for Minecraft agents.
Zenodo API metadata reports license id mit-license and total file bytes 8571175338.

candidates.name: Atari-HEAD human eye-tracking and demonstration dataset
candidates.published_size_note: Zenodo files total 8.7 GB decimal and include action_enums.txt plus per-game zips.
candidates.supervision: per-frame Atari action demonstrations with gaze/reward metadata
candidates.action_signal: ALE action integer/action enum per frame; gaze fields optional and not required for IDM/FDM action training
candidates.adapter_strategy: Use a discrete Atari action head and keep Atari metrics separated from D2E mouse/keyboard endpoint claims.
candidates.g005_role: small human-demonstration discrete-control auxiliary ablation and low-cost overfitting/control experiment
candidates.why: Human gameplay with explicit actions across 20 Atari games; useful for testing whether game-action pretraining helps heldout-game generalization.
risks: Atari observations/actions are lower dimensional than D2E desktop video
CC-BY attribution requirements must be propagated in artifact metadata
source_evidence: Zenodo v4 metadata includes action_enums.txt and per-game zips with cc-by-4.0 license id.
Atari-HEAD paper reports 117 gameplay hours, 20 games, 8M action demonstrations, and 328M gaze samples.

candidates.name: p-doom Atari-Breakout action-conditioned frames
candidates.published_size_note: Hugging Face file tree reports approximately 823 MB.
candidates.supervision: 10M 84x84 frames and actions collected from Atari Breakout agent training
candidates.action_signal: action-conditioned video prediction records with train/val/test splits
candidates.adapter_strategy: Use only for discrete-action data-loader/provenance tests and a small auxiliary scaling control; do not treat as human demonstration evidence.
candidates.g005_role: tiny permissive adapter/prototype corpus and negative/low-transfer control for aux scaling curves
candidates.why: Very small, permissive, and already split; useful to validate auxiliary ingestion without consuming cluster storage.
risks: ArrayRecord/Grain reader integration needed before training
source_evidence: Hugging Face dataset card lists license cc0-1.0, 10M frames/actions, 84x84 resolution, and train/val/test splits.
Hugging Face API reports dataset sha 34c60197acabf0011a36affff183cc87c755edc4 and card license cc0-1.0.

candidates.name: OpenAI VPT BASALT 2022 contractor demonstrations
candidates.published_size_note: Repository README states around 150 GB per BASALT task; four task indexes imply roughly 600 GB decimal before cache expansion.
candidates.license: repository code is MIT; data/license provenance requires explicit review before training use
candidates.supervision: MP4 recordings with associated JSONL action files/indexes for BASALT tasks
candidates.action_signal: keyboard/mouse-like Minecraft action JSONL paired with video demonstrations
candidates.adapter_strategy: If license review passes, use as the highest-priority Minecraft aux corpus with source-specific tokenizer and D2E finetune ablation.
candidates.g005_role: best high-transfer candidate after license/provenance review; not part of current selected working set
candidates.why: Closest public analog to VPT/FDM-style IDM labeling and behavioral cloning, with video+action contractor demonstrations.
risks: dataset terms are not as clearly packaged as Zenodo/HF candidates
around 150 GB per task and larger cache/checkpoint footprint
README notes action JSONL stripping/caveats for BASALT competition context
Minecraft IP disclaimer must be preserved
source_evidence: OpenAI VPT README documents IDM demo videos/actions and BASALT 2022 demonstration datasets around 150GB per task.
OpenAI VPT README says repository code is MIT but also includes a Minecraft IP disclaimer; dataset rights require separate review.
